name: Origin of the import
pageTitle: Add a reference number for this consignment
caption: About the consignment
heading: Origin of the import

secondaryActions.label: Save and review
secondaryActions.evidence: page-objects/notification/CountryOfOriginPage.ts:13-15 (btnSaveAndReview) — the country-of-origin control exposes a 'Save and review' primary button in the amend/review re-entry flow, in place of 'Save and continue'. Not observed in a happy-path trace.

secondaryActions.label: Save and return to hub
secondaryActions.evidence: page-objects/notification/CountryOfOriginPage.ts:17-19 (btnSaveAndReturnToHub) — alternative primary button rendered when the page is entered from the notification hub. Not observed in a happy-path trace.

structure.text: If the country of origin is not listed
Does your consignment require a region code?
Enter the region code / Enter up to 5 characters.
structure.govukComponent: Text input (revealed when Yes) — with custom country-code prefix widget

structure.text: Your internal reference number for this consignment (optional)

govukComponents.usage: Section caption 'About the consignment' above the H1
Country of origin (219 options, third countries only) and Country from where consigned (254 options, includes EU)
Expander 'If the country of origin is not listed' below Country of origin, explaining EU/EFTA countries are no longer selectable
Groups the 'Does your consignment require a region code?' Yes/No radios
Yes/No for 'Does your consignment require a region code?'; the Yes option reveals a conditional region-code input
Conditional region-code input (width-3, maxlength 3) revealed when Yes; internal reference number input (width-one-half)
'Enter up to 5 characters.' on the region code input; 'Enter any internal reference number...' on the internal reference number input

govukComponents.govukClass: n/a (rendered as a 'Back' link in the service-information banner, href='#')
govukComponents.usage: Back navigation to the previous page (reference number)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke inline prefix widget rendered immediately before the region-code text input. It shows the selected country's ISO code (e.g. 'AF') in region-code-box, followed by a '-' divider (region-code-divider), so the visible field reads like 'AF-___'. The actual <input name=region-code> only captures up to 3 trailing characters (maxlength=3); the country-code prefix is presentational/derived. The hint 'Enter up to 5 characters.' refers to the whole composed region code, not the input's own 3-char limit.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: region-code, region-code-box and region-code-divider are the only non-govuk classes on the page. This is a custom prefixed-input pattern with a dynamic prefix driven by the chosen country, plus a mismatch between the hint (5 chars) and the input's maxlength (3). The new app should model the region code as country-code + suffix explicitly rather than reproducing the DOM widget.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK 'Text input with prefix' (govuk-input__wrapper + govuk-input__prefix) covers the visual prefix cleanly; the dynamic country-code prefix and the composed-value logic are app concerns, not markup.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1, action 14 (.govuk-radios__conditional outerHTML). Corroboration: the 'AF' prefix equals the ISO code of the selected country of origin — types/country.ts:2 (Afghanistan: 'AF') — confirming the prefix is the country-of-origin ISO code, not free text.

fields.name: origin-country
fields.label: Country of origin
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — model @NotNull on Commodities.countryOfOrigin (ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/.../representation/Commodities.java:168-180) carries validation groups {HighRisk, Chedpp, CvedaEu, HighRiskEuCed, CvedpEu}. Of the CED-family groups only NotificationHighRiskEuCedFieldValidation (the high-risk-EU-CED variant) is present; the plain third-country CED groups applied by getCedValidationGroups (ipaffs-notification-microservice/.../validators/field/PartOneFieldValidator.java:156-174 → ProvideCtcMrn, GvmsRoute, NotificationSingleCedValidation) are NOT listed. So the legacy MODEL only hard-enforces country-of-origin for EU-CED, not for a standard third-country CHED-D. required=true is retained on the rendered/QA evidence (always selected); note the model gap — mandatoriness here is a policy the rebuild should confirm rather than inherit from the third-country CED annotation.
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1, action 14 (select name=origin-country, 219 options). Corroborated by page-objects/notification/CountryOfOriginPage.ts:6-8 (dropdownCountryOfOrigin = getByLabel('Country of origin')) and driven on every CHED-D journey by workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:200 — always selected, confirming required=true. Extra observedValues Australia + India are inferred: tests/notification/ched-d/ched-d-cuc.spec.ts:20 (country.Australia) and tests/notification/ched-d/ched-d-manipulation.spec.ts:44 (selectOption('India')). LEGACY: the CED-specific EU exclusion is server-driven — consignment_countries.js:38 filters origin countries to !country.eu for type===CED (HIGH_RISK_TYPES = [CVEDA, CVEDP, CED] at :30), and the CED-only 'not listed' Details partial is included at consignmentCountries.html:35-37. NOTE: in the QA-automation app version the country-of-origin dropdown lives on a SEPARATE preceding page ('Country of Origin Page') from the 'Origin of Import Page' — see openQuestions.

fields.name: region-code-option
fields.label: Does your consignment require a region code?
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — no @NotNull governs this control. It maps to the derived flag regionOfOriginOption (consignment_countries.js:163 = !isEmpty(regionOfOrigin)); the underlying model field Commodities.regionOfOrigin (Commodities.java:186-187) carries NO validation-group @NotNull, so region choice is optional at the model level. required=true is kept only in the sense that one radio is always pre-selected (No default), not that the user is forced to change it. DISCREPANCY: the current IPAFFS template gates this whole fieldset OFF for CED — see discrepancies[].
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1, action 14 (radios name=region-code-option; No checked by default). Corroborated: workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:204 and tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:34 submit the Origin-of-Import page with only a Save-and-continue click and no region-code interaction, consistent with 'No' being the pre-selected happy-path default. LEGACY default: consignmentCountries.html:95-101 pre-checks the No radio via {{#unless regionOfOriginOption}}checked{{/unless}}.

fields.name: region-code
fields.label: Enter the region code
fields.conditionalOn: Shown only when 'Does your consignment require a region code?' = Yes (govuk-radios__conditional reveal). Input is prefixed by the selected country's ISO code via a custom widget; the input itself has maxlength=3 (width-3).
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — Commodities.regionOfOrigin (Commodities.java:186-187) has no @NotNull under any group, so the region code is optional at the model level even when Yes is chosen; the '5 characters' is a hint, not an enforced min. No ValidationMessages.properties key exists for region-code (grep for 'region' returns nothing), confirming there is no server error copy for it. required stays false.
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1, action 14 (input name=region-code, maxlength=3, inside #conditional-region-code-yes). LEGACY: consignmentCountries.html:88-90 sets width/maxlength to 3 for non-CVEDA types (CVEDA gets 5); the label 'Enter the region code' (html:76-78, else-branch) and hint 'Enter up to 5 characters.' (html:80-82) match the rendered copy verbatim. No QA page object drives this field (the CHED-D happy-path workflow never selects Yes), so its required-when-Yes rule and validation copy remain a gap — see openQuestions. DISCREPANCY: current IPAFFS template gates the region-code fieldset OFF for CED — see discrepancies[].

fields.name: consigned-country
fields.label: Country from where consigned
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — model @NotNull on Commodities.consignedCountry (Commodities.java:189-201) carries validation groups {NotificationCedOrCvedpFieldValidation, Chedpp, NotificationHighRiskEuCedFieldValidation, CvedpEu}. NotificationCedOrCvedpFieldValidation IS a CED-family group by name, so consignedCountry is legacy-mandatory copy-wise (error 'Consigned country' exists). CAVEAT: getCedValidationGroups (PartOneFieldValidator.java:156-174) applies ProvideCtcMrn + GvmsRoute + NotificationSingleCedValidation for a standard third-country CED and does NOT add NotificationCedOrCvedpFieldValidation, so the @NotNull may only actually fire for the EU-CED variant (HighRiskEuCed). required=true retained on the rendered field + the existing validation message; flag the wiring nuance for the rebuild.
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1, action 14 (select name=consigned-country, 254 options — includes EU member states, unlike origin-country). LEGACY: consignmentCountries.html:106-119 renders this select for every type except CVEDA ({{^eq countryData.type "CVEDA"}}), sourced from the FULL list (data/countriesSelectHighLow countries=allCountries) — confirming the two distinct country lists (origin=third-country filtered, consigned=all). Placeholder option 'Select country from where consigned' is disabled (html:114-116). No QA page object exposes this control (the CHED-D happy-path workflow submits Origin-of-Import without touching it — ched-d-workflows.ts:204), which suggests it may be pre-populated from the country of origin rather than left blank; see openQuestions.

fields.name: local-reference-number
fields.label: Your internal reference number for this consignment (optional)
fields.hint: Enter any internal reference number you want to use to identify this consignment, or leave blank.
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — persisted as PartOne.importerLocalReferenceNumber (consignment_countries.js:161); no @NotNull and no ValidationMessages.properties key exists for it (grep returns nothing), so it is optional at the model level. required stays false.
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1, action 14 (input name=local-reference-number, govuk-input--width-one-half). LEGACY: consignmentCountries.html:176-186 renders the CED/CVEDA variant — labelText 'Your internal reference number for this consignment (optional)', hintText 'Enter any internal reference number you want to use to identify this consignment, or leave blank.' — both matching the rendered copy verbatim. (The else-branch for other types uses the different label 'Add a reference number for this consignment (optional)' — html:187-198 — which is where the leftover page <title> 'Add a reference number for this consignment' originates; see openQuestions.) Optional confirmed by the CHED-D happy-path workflow never filling it (ched-d-workflows.ts:204) yet progressing.

validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-notification-microservice/service/src/main/resources/ValidationMessages.properties:86 (uk.gov.defra.tracesx.notificationschema.representation.partone.commodities.countryoforigin.not.null=Country of origin). This is the @NotNull message for Commodities.countryOfOrigin; IPAFFS renders it as the validation-summary link / field error text. No rendered error trace captured it (both evidence traces are happy-path), so exact in-page presentation is unverified — a gap for an error-state CHED-D trace.

ipaffs-notification-microservice/service/src/main/resources/ValidationMessages.properties:87 (uk.gov.defra.tracesx.notificationschema.representation.partone.commodities.consignedcountry.not.null=Consigned country). @NotNull message for Commodities.consignedCountry. Not observed rendered (happy-path traces only).

No ValidationMessages.properties key exists for region-code / region-code-option (grep for 'region' returns nothing) and the model field Commodities.regionOfOrigin has no @NotNull. So there is no legacy server-side required rule or error copy for the region-code question or its revealed input — the required-when-Yes rule is undefined in the legacy source. Question for a human / error-state trace.
Optional field — no ValidationMessages.properties key and no @NotNull (grep for 'localreference'/'local.reference' returns nothing). No error copy exists by design.

discrepancies: REGION-CODE FIELDSET — template vs rendered. The current IPAFFS template gates the entire region-code question OFF for CED: consignmentCountries.html:40 wraps the fieldset in {{#if (not (eq notification.type 'CHEDPP') (eq notification.type 'CED'))}} (a custom @budibase/handlebars-helpers 'not' subexpression whose two-arg semantics are non-obvious), i.e. it is intended to render only for types that are neither CHEDPP nor CED. Yet trace b3742f19...action 14 rendered region-code-option + region-code for a CED (type=CED) notification. Rendered reality wins — the region-code fields are KEPT as confirmed — but the divergence means either (a) the traced app version predates the CED exclusion (IPAFFS source has since moved to drop region-code for CHED-D), or (b) the helper does not evaluate as it reads. RECONCILE: decide whether CHED-D should carry a region-code question at all; the legacy trajectory appears to be removing it.
openQuestions: The <title> element (document.title) reads 'Add a reference number for this consignment' on the origin-of-import page in BOTH evidence traces — it does NOT match the H1 'Origin of the import'. This is either a genuine IPAFFS page-title bug (title left over from the previous page-2 template) or a snapshot artefact. Treat the browser-tab title as a gap for the rebuild; do not copy the mismatch.

Country of origin (origin-country) offers 219 options and deliberately EXCLUDES EU/EFTA member states (no Austria, Belgium, France, Germany, etc.); the Details expander explains 'You can no longer select the EU and European Free Trade Association (EFTA) countries from this list.' Country from where consigned (consigned-country) offers 254 options and DOES include EU states. The new app must source two DISTINCT country lists from reference data (third-country vs full list), not one.

No validation was captured on this page — both evidence traces are happy-path (0 errors). The QA automation adds no error assertion for this page either (ched-d tests only assert the amended country-of-origin value on the review page — ched-d-manipulation.spec.ts:55,66). Required-field error copy for origin-country / consigned-country / region-code-option (and region-code when Yes) is therefore still a gap; mine an error-state CHED-D trace to confirm.

The Details expander links to https://www.gov.uk/guidance/import-high-risk-food-and-feed-not-of-animal-origin-from-the-eu-to-great-britain (link text 'Read more on EU and EFTA countries', opens in new tab) — this confirms the page is CHED-D/HRFNAO-specific.

'Does your consignment require a region code?' defaults to 'No' pre-selected. Whether that default is intended, and the exact region-code composition rule (country ISO prefix + up to 3 chars vs the '5 characters' hint), needs confirming. The trace-observed 'AF' prefix equals the ISO code of the selected country of origin (types/country.ts:2), confirming the prefix is country-derived, but the required-when-Yes rule and max-length semantics are unverified.

TWO-PAGE ORIGIN STRUCTURE — confirmed in the trace, matching the QA automation (earlier claim of a 'single page' was wrong). The trace renders the origin stage as TWO consecutive pages: (1) 'Origin of the animal or product' at /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/consignment-origin?type=CED, a single 'Country of origin' select and nothing else (trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1, action 12 after-snapshot — sole real control is select name=origin-country), which matches QA's CountryOfOriginPage.ts (driven at ched-d-workflows.ts:199-201; ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:27-30); then (2) THIS page, 'Origin of the import' at consignment/page-2 (trace b3742..., action 14 INPUT-phase snapshot; corroborated by eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f, action 16), which REPEATS its own origin-country select and adds region-code-option, region-code, consigned-country and local-reference-number, submitted with a bare Save-and-continue (ched-d-workflows.ts:203-204; accessibility spec:32-34). So the QA two-page split IS the rendered reality; the wrinkle is that page-2 carries a SECOND origin-country select redundant with the preceding consignment-origin page. NOTE: QA's OriginOfImportPage.ts models three radio groups that appear on NEITHER trace page — see openQuestion below (cross-type page-object leakage). The rebuild must decide whether the two origin-country selects are genuinely duplicated or a legacy artefact.

URL / PAGE-NUMBER CAVEAT: this page is consignment/page-2, NOT page-3. In both evidence traces the Save-and-continue click ON this page navigates TO consignment/page-3, which is the COMMODITY search page (trace b3742..., action 14 navigates to page-3 then action 15 fills 'Enter commodity code', document.title 'Search Commodities'; same in eddc..., action 16). Do not confuse page-3 (Commodity) with this origin-of-import page. IMPORTANT extraction detail for anyone re-verifying: the origin-of-import DOM lives ONLY in the action's INPUT-phase snapshot (the click actions on this page have no before/after snapshot in the trace) — use 'trace snapshot 14 --name input' (b3742...) / 'trace snapshot 16 --name input' (eddc...), not before/after.

page-objects/notification/OriginOfImportPage.ts:6-15 exposes THREE radio groups on its 'Origin of Import' page that appear in NO CHED-D trace in the corpus: 'Does this consignment conform' (Yes), 'Does your consignment require a health certificate?' (No), and 'Will the consignment change [after BCP]' (Yes/'Will the consignment change'). These were NOT injected as fields because they directly contradict the confirmed trace composition (rendered reality wins). They may belong to a different CHED type reusing the same page object, or to a newer app version. Flag for a human: are these controls part of the CHED-D Origin-of-Import requirement, or cross-type page-object leakage? LEGACY PARTIAL RESOLUTION: at least the 'Will the consignment change ... after the Border Control Post (BCP)?' radio (transport-details-required) is CVEDP-ONLY in the legacy source — consignment_countries.js:165 sets showTransportDetailsRequired = (notification.type === CVEDP), and consignmentCountries.html:166-175 renders it only under that flag. It is therefore NOT part of the CHED-D (CED) Origin-of-Import page — confirming that group as cross-type page-object leakage. Similarly 'Does this consignment conform to regulatory requirements?' (conform-uk-regulations) is CVEDP-only — consignmentCountries.html:122 gates it on {{#eq countryData.type "CVEDP"}}. So two of the three leaked radio groups are legacy-confirmed CVEDP-only; the health-certificate radio remains unexplained.

The country-of-origin control is amendable post-creation via an 'Amend country of origin' link (page-objects/notification/AmendNotificationPage.ts linkAmendCountryOfOrigin, used at ched-d-manipulation.spec.ts:42-45), and re-entry can render 'Save and review' / 'Save and return to hub' primary buttons instead of 'Save and continue' (CountryOfOriginPage.ts:13-19). The rebuild must support editing this page from the hub/review, not only in the forward journey.
